title: Brain Protocol v2 registry entry
description: One system's entry in the conformance registry (BP-09 §4.1, normative shape). Entries are signed by the registry key; the registry is append-only in history (status changes are events, not overwrites), publicly readable, and carries no telemetry — it records attestations given to it, it observes nothing (CD-3).

card_url.description: The system's signed agent card at its well-known path (BP-03 §2.1).

card_key_fingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the card-signing identity key; peers verify the served card matches (BP-09 §4.2).

tier.description: Class D with no S2 capability self-certifies; Classes A/H and any S2 capability require verified certification (CD-2, BP-09 §3.1).

signatures.description: Required for tier 'verified': the suite editor plus a certified Class H peer (BP-09 §3.1).

expires.description: Verified certificates expire 24 months after issue (BP-09 §3.2); self-certification expires at the next MAJOR suite version.

kit_commit.description: Exact kit build the results cite (results.json kit_commit).

results_url.description: The full machine-readable results, published at a stable URL (schemas/tck-results.schema.json).

s2_capabilities.description: S2 capabilities offered; any entry here requires tier 'verified' (CD-2).

revocation.description: Set when status is revoked (BP-09 §4.3). Revocation stands during appeal — fail closed, not open.
